response: Let's take a step back and try a different approach.

It is completely normal to feel confused by Data Structures and Algorithms because these concepts are very abstract when you first see them. Please do not worry, as almost every computer science student struggles with this topic at the beginning.

To make these concepts easier to grasp, try drawing the data structures like trees or linked lists on paper and tracing how the data moves step-by-step. Seeing how the algorithms work visually makes the actual code much easier to understand.

As a great next step, pick just one simple structure today, like an array or a linked list, and practice writing a basic program to add and remove items from it. Taking it one small concept at a time will help you build your confidence and master the material.

It is completely natural to feel overwhelmed by complex computer science concepts, but your determination to tackle it shows great strength.

When you feel stuck, try writing out the problem in plain English pseudocode before you attempt to write any actual code. This allows you to focus on the logic of the solution without worrying about programming syntax.

For our next step, share the specific topic or question you are working on, and we can break down the very first part of it together.

It is wonderful that you are approaching this physics problem with curiosity, as being curious is the best way to learn. Having a starting confidence of 59% is a great place to begin exploring and building your understanding.

A great strategy for physics is to always draw a quick sketch of the situation and list your known variables first. This helps you visualize what is happening before you try to solve any equations.

Go ahead and share the physics question with me now. We can work through it together one step at a time to build up your confidence.
